title: Google’s ‘Non-Commodity’ Push Isn’t New
description:
Like EEAT before it, non-commodity content is not easily summarized or recycled by generative AI without attribution.
The post Google’s ‘Non-Commodity’ Push Isn’t New appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: Taboola expands DeeperDive into an ad network for AI apps and agents
description: The company sees a larger opportunity in powering advertising across conversational experiences.

title: WPP forecasts AI search ads to become fastest growing channel in advertising
description: Twin projections from holdco WPP  and Madison & Wall, peg rising search spend amid a resilient ad economy, which is still expected to grow despite headwinds.

title: AI forces retailers, brands to rethink their product pages
description: Leaders across e-commerce are transforming how they approach designing product pages to be more discoverable to AI agents like ChatGPT, Claude and Gemini.

title: Tractor Supply taps AI to help scale last-mile delivery network
description: The retailer has used the technology to help managers build delivery routes, and more AI-driven improvements are in the works.

title: Why do South Koreans love AI so much?
description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. When I landed in Seoul after a grueling 12-hour flight from San Francisco, I walked through an unmanned immigration checkpoint, where a machine scanned my face and passport. On the subway home,…

title: EA rolls out advertising platform with enhanced offerings for brands
description: The video game company is enabling brands, including initial partners Coach and Visa, to create, launch and measure campaigns across its library.

title: #219: Claude Fable 5, OpenAI IPO, Apple Siri AI Finally Unveiled & Is the Era of Affordable AI Over?
description:
The U.S. government forced Anthropic to pull Fable 5 and Mythos 5 from general availability just days after they launched as the most capable models publicly available. Paul and Mike work through how it happened, Dario Amodei's policy essay, and the question every business leader should be sitting with: what do you build on when a model can be switched off?

Then it's OpenAI's confidential IPO filing, Apple's long-awaited Siri AI, the real economics behind your AI subscription, and a DeepMind paper on the road from AGI to ASI.

Show Notes: Access the show notes and show links here

title: Ad Tech Briefing: The shifting tides behind Publicis and The Trade Desk’s détente
description: Plus, the five key questions marketers have to ask about AI platform pitches.

title: [AINews] Satya on Loopcraft: Building Frontier Ecosystems
description: a quiet day lets us report on Satya's hit essay

title: Why more better-for-you brands are leading with taste over health claims
description: Better-for-you brands are prioritizing taste and fun packaging to attract more customers while still offering healthier food options.

title: Rising fuel costs push brands to adjust shipping policies
description: Brands are raising free shipping thresholds, adding new delivery fees and testing paid shipping options as higher fuel costs push up the price of moving goods across supply chains.

title: Malaysia’s AI agent-powered messaging app Respond.io raises $62.5M, eyes acquisitions
description: Respond.io, one of Malaysia startups to watch, uses AI agents to handle high volumes of customer inquiries and charges per convo, not per seat.

title: Dick’s plots 100 Lids shop-in-shops by the end of the summer
description: A partnership featuring licensed and lifestyle headwear is already available at 46 locations and will expand nationwide in the months to come.

title: Target’s Brian Cornell to remain executive chairman after proposal rejected
description: Shareholders did not approve a policy suggestion making the board chair an independent director, despite pushback on Cornell's appointment.
